question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any planned remedial works for the A6, from Maghera to Dungiven, in the next two years.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and this has had a significant impact on its ability to deliver essential services such as Structural Maintenance. Regrettably, for some time the available investment for my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme has not kept pace with what is needed to maintain all of our infrastructure in a reasonable condition.While there are many carriageways across the road network, including the A6, which would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, structural maintenance work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.  Details of the planned list of carriageway and footway resurfacing schemes for the 2025/26 financial year will be included within my Department’s Annual Report to Councils.In the meantime, officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any planned capital works to libraries in the Foyle constituency.
answer_text: The Department for Communities continues to support Libraries NI to develop and invest in the network of the five public libraries in the Foyle constituency.Libraries NI has, over recent years, delivered capital infrastructure enhancement projects at Creggan, Shantallow, Strathfoyle and Waterside libraries.  At present there are no planned capital works to libraries in the Foyle constituency; however, I am given to understand that Libraries NI are currently undertaking a feasibility study which will inform potential future development proposals for the Derry Central Library facility.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the schools in the Foyle constituency that will be included in the Safer Routes to School initiative.
answer_text: A review of the initiative introducing part-time 20mph speed limits at schools has been undertaken and is available on my Department’s internet site Review of Part-Time 20mph Speed Limits at Schools | Department for Infrastructure.The review recommends that the Department should provide a third tranche of part-time 20 mph speed limits at schools, however this will be more modest in size and is dependent on the availability of funding. The report also concludes that a more targeted approach is needed to ensure that the correct safety intervention is selected and work updating our policy document is underway.   Funding for progressing the next tranche of part-time 20 mph speed limits at schools will be included within budget planning considerations for next financial year.I would also like to see what can be done to improve attitudes to speeding, not only for road safety purposes, but to make places cleaner and greener.  As part of my consideration of the recently published review I have asked my officials to prepare a paper on 20mph speed limits in general taking into consideration schools, residential areas and places where there is a higher number of those walking, wheeling and cycling.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in light of the December 2024 NISRA report Alcohol-Specific Deaths in Northern Ireland 2023 that shows that alcohol-specific deaths in Northern Ireland have risen 65.5 per cent since 2013, to detail the actions his Department is taking to (i) raise awareness of; and (ii) address alcohol-specific illnesses.
answer_text: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) is currently evaluating givinostat (Duvyzat®) for the treatment of Duchenne muscular dystrophy in people of 6 years and over, with final guidance expected to publish in July 2025.  In Northern Ireland, the paediatric neuromuscular service is delivered by the Belfast Heath and Social Care Trust who have responsibility for participation in the Early Access Programmes for givinostat.  Whilst the manufacturer has made givinostat available via an Early Access Programme (EAP) for eligible patients in parts of the UK, the Belfast Trust has advised that it is unfortunately not in a position to facilitate the EAP due to the associated resource implications.The South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust will however monitor the outcomes of the programme, and any potential roll out to the adult population. When the final guidance for this treatment is issued by NICE, my Department will consider it under the usual process for NICE Technology Appraisals as per the guidance set out in circular HSC (SQSD) 12/22 which is available at: https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/health/doh-hsc-sqsd-12-22.pdf

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the amount spent on locum doctors in each of the last three years, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when her Department plans to resurface the Strabane Old Road between Newbuildings and Gobnascale, Londonderry.
answer_text: While there are many roads across the Foyle constituency, including the Strabane Old Road, that would benefit from investment, due to over a decade of austerity and underfunding by the British Government, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.  Details of the planned list of resurfacing schemes for the 2025/26 financial year will be included within my Department’s Annual Report to Councils.In the meantime, officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s policy

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the legal position on the use of e-scooters on public (i) roads; and (ii) spaces in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: It is currently illegal to use e-scooters on public roads and public spaces here and I have no plans to change that position. Anyone using an e-scooter illegally may be subject to enforcement action by the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline what steps her Department is taking to ensure the workforce has the required skills to adapt to Artificial Intelligence advancements.
To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department intends to have further consultation with key stakeholders on her Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: My Department held a public consultation last year, seeking views on how best to strengthen the North’s employment law framework. Although the formal consultation period has closed, engagement continues to take place on an ongoing basis as we shape necessary updates to our employment law framework.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the sale of the former Faughan Valley High School site in Drumahoe.
answer_text: In November 2024, a potential purchaser withdrew from the sale prior to signing an agreement. In January 2025 the Education Authority’s estate agent identified another potential purchaser. The legal due diligence process is underway again and it is hoped that EA will be in a contract before the end of the financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any steps her Department is taking to improve pedestrian safety on the Foyle Bridge, Londonderry.
answer_text: My Department regularly reviews the road network to identify potential interventions to improve pedestrian and road user safety. While no general pedestrian safety issues have been identified on the Foyle Bridge from a network development perspective, I recognise the wider health objectives of improving the safety for vulnerable individuals who use the bridge.  Within this wider context, raising the parapets of the bridge has been discussed at the Cross Departmental Coordinated Action Group.  However, before any work can be carried out on the bridge, a detailed structural model will need to be developed to ensure that any changes to the parapets do not compromise the structural integrity of the bridge. The model is estimated to cost around £420,000, and the subsequent works to install a barrier may cost in the region of £10m.My Department will continue to work with all stakeholders to identify funding to complete the structural assessment to determine if increasing the barrier heights at Foyle Bridge is possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount her Department has spent replacing or repairing damaged road signage with the word Londonderry on it in the last three years.
answer_text: My Department does not keep specific records of how traffic signs have been damaged, only that they are damaged.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any action her Department is taking to address (i) traffic congestion; and (ii) pedestrian safety at the Dales Corner and Limavady Road junction, Londonderry.
answer_text: Significant junction improvement work is planned at Dales Corner commencing in Summer 2025.  This work will involve an upgrade to the traffic signals, changes to the existing lane markings to increase junction capacity, and the realignment of the road to facilitate the widening of the narrow footway in front of Ebrington Terrace.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any financial support his Department is providing to address the damage caused to forests during Storm Éowyn.
answer_text: Whilst there is no financial support available from DAERA to support woodland owners for damage caused by Storm Éowyn my Department’s Forest Service provide grant aid for the replanting of woodlands following felling. Details of the grant, which is called the Woodland Investment Grant (Restocking), are available via the Department’s website https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/topics/grants-and-funding.The website also provides advice aimed at supporting woodland owners to manage the impacts of storm damage to their woodlands with information about tree safety, felling licence requirements and how to contact professional forestry agents and contractors for operational capability.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the impact of Inheritance Tax changes on the agriculture industry in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The changes announced in the Budget to limit agricultural property relief and business property relief combined to £1m for 100% relief from 6 April 2026 have caused great concern in the agricultural community and will have significant adverse impacts for farming families in Northern Ireland.In December 2024, my Department, undertook analysis of the potential impact of the inheritance tax changes on local farms. This analysis was based on land values but also took account for buildings, residential property, livestock, and machinery. The analysis found that just under half of all farms in NI have agricultural and business property values exceeding £1 million using an average valuation of £21,000/acre. Furthermore, these farms account for around 80% of the total land farmed. Therefore, the impact of the inheritance tax policy due to come into effect in April 2026 have the potential to have significant impacts in Northern Ireland. There are actions that farmers can take to reduce future tax liability but these will depend on individual circumstances and it is difficult to estimate how much this will mitigate against the overall impact of the policy change. I would encourage farmers to seek tax advice from an appropriate professional before making any decisions.Within my remit as DAERA Minister, I will do all I can to continue to support the farming community in Northern Ireland on this issue. However, as taxation it is not a devolved matter, this does place limitations on the measures that I can take. I would urge the UK Government to look at how inheritance tax operates in other European Countries, particularly Germany where agricultural land obtains up to 100% relief if it is retained in agricultural use and not sold by the inheritor within a set period of time (7 years in Germany).
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the current status of the Londonderry Riverfront, Walled City, Ebrington and Craft Village public realm scheme.
answer_text: The current status of the listed projects is as follows:Londonderry Central Riverfront projectThe £45 million Londonderry Central Riverfront project (a City Deal and Inclusive Future Fund project) is being led by the Department for Infrastructure.  The project includes the introduction of a new two-lane, one-way active travel corridor along Strand Road and Queen’s Quay and the redesign of the central riverfront area reallocating road space to make it more pedestrian and cycle friendly.  Whilst DfI is the lead Department, DfC has an interest given the project’s regeneration and placemaking elements. Walled City Regeneration project My Department is currently considering for approval Council’s Outline Business Case for the £14.7 million Walled City Regeneration project (a City Deal and Inclusive Future Fund project). Works include reconfiguration of the Tower Museum, a Lighting Enhancement Plan and high-quality public realm improvements.Londonderry North Atlantic Museum at Ebrington SquareMy Department is contributing £500,000 capital funding towards the Londonderry North Atlantic Museum in Ebrington Square.Craft Village projectIt is understood that the Walled City Regeneration project will provide linkages to a separate Craft Village project which is to be funded and delivered separately.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any financial support her Department is providing to the Derry North Atlantic Maritime Museum, Ebrington.
answer_text: The Derry on the North Atlantic (DNA) Maritime Museum is a key project within the Derry City and Strabane District Council (DCSDC) City Deal.  My Department is a part funder of the project, accountable for an investment of £5.3m funded through the Inclusive Futures Fund.  My Department looks forward to continuing to work closely with Derry City and Strabane District Council and other partners to ensure the delivery of all substantial outcomes, contributing to the wider aim of sustainably growing the tourism industry in the North and therefore a more regionally balanced economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps his Department is taking to reduce gastroenterology waiting lists in the Western Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The Western Trust has undertaken a number of actions to address waiting times for gastroenterology.It has recently advertised and successfully recruited to a fully funded Consultant Gastroenterologist post.Waiting lists have been validated and patients have been referred directly for tests as clinically appropriate.Clinic outpatient templates have been reviewed to prioritise the longest waiting patients where possible.Waiting List Initiative (WLI) and Independent Sector (IS) providers have been utilised as funded.  There has been a recent focus on Red Flag and Urgent Patient waiting lists that have seen a marked reduction in waiting time.  The current focus is on patient reviews.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the membership of the Local Economic Partnerships that have been established, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: Membership of the Local Economic Partnerships (LEP) is for to councils to determine. Proposed membership will be reviewed and approved by the Department prior to funding being released from April 2025. As all LEPs have not finalised their membership, I cannot provide a breakdown of membership. 		The Department issued guidance on membership to councils in November 2024, this included a list of mandatory and proposed members for each Local Economic Partnership (LEP).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline what steps he is taking to support the hospitality sector in Northern Ireland following plans by the Irish Government to reduce its VAT rate for hospitality to nine per cent.
To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the establishment of the Local Economic Partnerships.
answer_text: The Department and Invest NI have been working with councils and other stakeholders over the past months to establish Local Economic Partnerships (LEPs). Overall good progress has been made, some LEPs are already meeting in January with the others planned to follow in February and March. 	I formally announced a breakdown of funding allocations at Newry Mourne and Down’s inaugrual Local Economic Partnership meeting on 27th January. Details of fund allocations and rationale can be found on the following webpage  Regional balance fund | Department for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the amount of funding that will be made available to Derry City and Strabane District Council under the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Local Change Fund.
answer_text: Derry City & Strabane District Council will receive total funding of £165,000. This includes allocations under both the Momentum Fund 2024/25 element (£50,000) and the Local Change Fund for 2025/26 (£115,000).

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide a list of the consultee responses to his proposed Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: A summary and breakdown of the respondents to the Good Jobs Consultation will be published with the Departmental Response Document.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline what actions her Department is taking to support businesses following the Chancellor's Autumn Budget 2024.
To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department has any plans to ensure patients do not miss medical appointments arising from delays with their postal delivery.
answer_text: Each Trust has mitigating actions in place to ensure patients are advised of their outpatient appointments in a timely manner.In the Western Trust, all Departments within Unscheduled Care and Medicine, Cancer and Diagnostics are fully aware of the current delays within the Post office and are making alternative arrangements such as text alerts, telephone contact etc.The Belfast Trust has been holding ongoing and regular meetings in place with the provider to monitor the service provision, address any issues which may arise and mitigate against service failures.As part of the Integrated Elective Access Policy (IEAP) that applies to all Trusts, Northern Trust patients are invited to call the Trust to agree an outpatient appointment and this is followed up by an appointment letter confirming the date and time of the appointment.  An interim process has been put in place to contact all patients, by telephone, receiving an appointment with less than two weeks’ notice.  The South Eastern Trust acute specialties issue text reminders about appointments. While, Mental Health Outpatients do not routinely issue reminders. Contact by telephone occurs for those people known to services who benefit from encouragement to attend. It is envisaged that MyCare will be used to keep people updated re appointments..It is not unexpected that issues may arise due to postal delivery, this can occur at any point, however, it is important to be reassured that HSC Trusts are taking proactive measures to make sure any potential delays are mitigated with contingency plans.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the financial support provided by his Department to Derry City and Strabane District Council to meet the 70 per cent recycling target, as set out in the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: Whilst not solely aimed at meeting the Climate Change Act target, my Department’s £23m Household Waste Recycling Collaborative Change Programme (HWRCCP) assists local councils to transform kerbside recycling and Household Recycling Centre infrastructure and services to help increase recycling and deliver a circular economy in Northern Ireland. Derry City and Strabane District Council were awarded £852,247 from the HWRCCP in 2020 for their project to extend a garden waste collection service to an additional 25,000 properties and to provide out-centres such as leisure and community centres with indoor recycling bins. In addition, my Department has been engaging frequently with all eleven local councils to ensure Northern Ireland is best placed to meet the ambitious recycling targets set in legislation including the 70 per cent recycling target, as set out in the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.  We have recently collaborated with Derry City & Strabane District Council to model a range of potential waste collection scenarios, aimed at optimising waste collections and improving the quality and quantity of recycling across the district. My Department also provides funding to the Waste & Resources Action Programme (WRAP) to provide essential local council support, including specialist technical guidance, as well as delivering key national and regional behaviour change campaigns.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the tourism budget in each of the last three financial years (i) allocated to Tourism NI and spent directly on tourism in the Derry City and Strabane District Council area; and (ii) allocated to Derry City and Strabane District Council to distribute on tourism within the area.
answer_text: allocated to Tourism NI and spent directly on tourism in the Derry City and Strabane District Council area; The vast majority of Tourism NI’s budget goes on regional wide initiatives from which the Derry City and Strabane Council area benefits along with other regions. These initiatives include promotional and business support activity that takes place across a number of council areas, for which specific council area figures cannot be disaggregated. These include activities such as familiarisation trips by journalists and tour operators, training events and fully integrated marketing campaigns that take place twice a year in Spring and Autumn. As a result, the figures provided below relate to direct spend solely in the Derry City and Strabane District Council area, excluding broader regional activity that the council benefits from:2023/24 - £638,6972022/23 - £331,1032021/22 - £313,773allocated to Derry City and Strabane District Council to distribute on tourism within the area.	The amounts provided directly to Derry City and Strabane District Council to support tourism in their area over the past three years is:2023/24 - £311,8832022/23 - £338,7132021/22 - £807,634

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the (i) number; and (ii) value of successful applications to the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme in the Foyle constituency.
answer_text: As of 17 January 2025, childcare providers have submitted claims for 382 children in the Foyle Constituency, with a value of £153,041.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on plans for a new MOT test centre in the Foyle constituency.
answer_text: The Driver and Vehicle Agency is finalising the design, costs and a planning application for a new MOT test centre on the McLean Road in the Campsie Industrial Estate. Approval to proceed with the proposals will be subject to further consideration and funding availability.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the steps his Department and Translink are taking to improve public transport links to Ulster University Magee campus.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:Translink are represented on Ulster University Travel and Transport Working Group who meet quarterly to promote and develop sustainable transport links to all Ulster University campuses.   The Magee campus is well served by existing public transport services with 15 services per hour between 7am-9am and 8 services per hour off-peak.   Translink’s Marketing team, work with Ulster University to promote public transport during their Welcome Week and throughout the academic year.   Costings to provide a free shuttle bus between the Northwest Transport Hub, Foyle Street Bus Station and the Magee Campus have been shared with Ulster University and discussions relating to this are ongoing. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how his Department is planning to review its approach to funding of commissioned places, including the per-student resource, paid to universities.
answer_text: I have asked my officials to consider possible future changes to the funding of healthcare programmes for which my department is responsible.  This work will address funding models and take into account a range of views from stakeholders, including educational providers, as well as the evidence from the approaches taken in other jurisdictions.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether their Department plans to continue providing funding for security personnel at the Ebrington site in the 2025-26 financial year.
answer_text: Guard security was put in place at Ebrington when the site was largely vacant and under development.  Today, Ebrington is thriving and much like the city centre, is a hub of activity with a wide range of both day and night-time businesses on the site who have their own security provision in place. From April 2025, security guard provision at the Ebrington site will cease. The site will continue to be monitored 24 hours a day via the existing extensive CCTV network.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the development of the new model of delivery for further education colleges.
answer_text: My officials have commenced work on a co-designed Further Education Transformation programme which is expected to be delivered over the next 24 months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the timescale for the publication of the Offshore Renewable Energy Action Plan.
answer_text: It is anticipated that the most recent iteration of the OREAP will be published alongside the upcoming public consultation on the Strategic Environmental Assessment/Habitats Regulations Assessment of the North’s marine area. This public consultation has been delayed as there are spatial matters that need to be resolved with the Ministry of Defence. The process that we are working through now will have benefits for the future and ensure a sustainable approach for offshore renewable energy development in the North. The Department and our key stakeholders continue to work collaboratively towards delivery of the actions as outlined within the draft OREAP.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the steps his Department is taking to provide funding support for the Community Crisis Intervention Service in Londonderry beyond March 2025.
answer_text: Extern's Community Crisis Service was previously provided funding through the Mental Health Support Fund. That funding stream has now ended for all recipient organisations. £42,300 non-recurrent slippage funding was identified for Extern's Community Crisis Service until the end of March 2025. The Budget settlement for future years has not yet been agreed and so it is not possible to definitively answer your question. However, I can advise it is likely Health and Social Care (HSC) will be facing a significant funding shortfall in 2025-26. Although work continues across the HSC system to deliver the maximum possible savings and efficiencies, due to the challenging financial position it is likely there will not be sufficient funding available to me to extend or replace this funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the roll-out of the Renewable Electricity Support Scheme.
answer_text: Action 17 in my Department’s 2024/2025 Business Plan has a target date of March 2025 to publish the final design of a renewable electricity support scheme for the North.		A first auction is anticipated in early 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the plans his Department has for the former park and ride site at Drumahoe, Londonderry.
answer_text: My department plans to have the former park and ride site restored to its original condition in compliance with the original planning permission.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how his Department considers areas of deprivation when allocating funding to Health and Social Care Trusts.
answer_text: The Public Health Agency (PHA) who are responsible for population screening programmes in Northern Ireland have advised that the information requested is not available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the steps his Department plans to take to address ongoing flooding issues on Church Road, Londonderry.
answer_text: Following a period of very heavy rainfall on 23rd July 2022 a culvert inlet screen on the designated Glendermott Stream became blocked with debris resulting in flooding that affected Church Road and Ivy Mead Mews.  My Department upgraded the culvert inlet screen in December 2023 to reduce the likelihood of flooding occurring at this location again.My Department will continue its inspection and maintenance regime in relation to designated watercourses and their associated infrastructure within this area. If an inspection determines that there is a blockage significantly impeding the free flow of water within the watercourse or that drainage infrastructure is in an unsatisfactory condition, then works will be programmed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the legislation he plans to introduce before the end of the 2022-2027 mandate.
answer_text: The legislation I plan to introduce before the end of the mandate is as follows:Decarbonisation Utility Regulator Powers, Non-domestic RHI Scheme Closure, Onshore Petroleum Licensing, Insolvency (Amendment) Bill, Good Jobs (Employment Rights), Energy – Offshore Renewable Energy Installations, Energy – Renewable Electricity Support Scheme, Energy Bill and Credit Union Bill.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the schools in the Foyle constituency that will benefit from his announcement on funding for (i) outdoor play equipment; and (ii) new desktop computers.
answer_text: Every grant-aided pre-school, nursery and primary school in the Foyle constituency and across Northern Ireland will receive funding for outdoor play equipment.  The level of funding received will be dependent on pupil numbers and details of the scheme will be provided to schools and settings over coming weeks.All post primary schools in the Foyle constituency will receive an allocation of desktop computers to replace those which have reached their end of life to ensure that students have access to secure, modern technology.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to outline what steps are being taken to improve the safety and security of MLAs and their constituency office staff.
To ask the Minister for Communities to detail what learning his Department can take from the Public Accounts Committee report on Child Poverty in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I welcome the Public Account Committee’s interest in this important policy area. My officials are considering the content of the report and will respond to the recommendations in due course under the established Memorandum of Reply protocol. It would not be appropriate to comment further at this stage.Addressing poverty in all its forms is a key priority for me. As poverty is a complex and multi-faceted issue, my Department is currently engaging with officials from other Departments to develop an Anti-Poverty Strategy and a range of actions which will help mitigate impacts, reduce the risk of falling into poverty and support people to exit poverty.I will bring the draft Strategy to the Executive in early 2025 for their consideration. Following Executive agreement of this paper the Strategy will be subject to public consultation prior to its final publication.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current budget his Department has allocated to delivering on the Mental Health Strategy 2021-2031.
answer_text: The Mental Health Strategy 2021-2031 was published alongside a 10-year Funding Plan. The Funding Plan outlined that £1.2bn is needed to fully implement the Strategy over the 10-year period. The investment required to deliver the Strategy is in addition to existing expenditure in mental health services.To date, in the absence of additional funding, the Department has been using its existing funding to progress implementation of the Strategy. While this funding has allowed the commencement of enabling actions and scope to take forward work on some other priorities, it does not match the levels of funding as set out in the Funding Plan.  In the current year, £5.9m has been allocated against the £42m envisaged in the Funding Plan.  It is clear that mental health services require more funding.  In the absence of a budget beyond the current year it is not possible to make any firm plans to increase overall spending on HSC services.  It is my intention to look at longer term planning for the HSC as a whole to see how best services can be delivered to enable them to be put onto a sustainable footing and enable unmet need to be addressed.  Planning for mental health spending will be an integral part of that process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the steps his Department is taking to deliver an anti-poverty strategy.
answer_text: I remain committed to developing effective and, just as importantly, sustainable solutions to help mitigate impacts, reduce the risk of falling into poverty and support people in exiting poverty through the implementation of a robust Anti-Poverty Strategy.I have written to Ministers of the other Departments and a Cross Departmental Working Group has been established. Senior officials across all Departments are engaging in work to identify key actions that will have a meaningful and lasting impact on socio-economic disadvantage in Northern Ireland.  I intend this work to be taken forward at pace, presenting a paper on the Anti-Poverty Strategy to the Executive early in 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of passengers using the Londonderry to Coleraine rail service and onwards to Belfast in each of the last three years.
answer_text: In the 2022-23 period the passenger figures for the Derry/Londonderry line was 2,436,607.In the 2023-24 period the passenger figures for the Derry/Londonderry line were 2,743,654.In the 2024-25 period the passenger figures for the Derry/Londonderry line so far is 1,306,309.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department intends to increase Invest NI's total spending in the Derry City and Strabane District Council area as part of his Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: The focus of my economic vision is about growing the economy across the North. This will require increased investment by Invest NI in all areas.My Department and Invest NI recognise the need to do more in Derry City and Strabane District council to improve the business base, to grow productivity performance and to encourage higher productivity businesses in the area. This imperative is reflected in the Sub-Regional Economic Plan and Invest NI’s draft 2024/25 – 26/27 Business Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline what steps her Department is taking to help eradicate violence against women and girls.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department plans to improve the pedestrian and cycle infrastructure between Bready and Newbuildings, Londonderry, in the absence of the new A5 Western Transport Corridor progressing in that area.
answer_text: Officials in my Department are working closely with councils to better understand their five-year greenway programme, and to develop an appropriate grant and support framework for the effective delivery of such programmes. The route between Newbuildings and Bready is being considered as part of this process, and officials will continue to collaborate with Derry City and Strabane District Council on the development of greenways and active travel routes.As part of my Department’s work on encouraging active travel, officials will also continue to identify further opportunities to improve the pedestrian and cycle infrastructure in the area.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will consider bringing forward legislation to lower the age limit for prostate cancer testing.
answer_text: Screening for prostate cancer is currently not recommended by the UK National Screening Committee (UK NSC), an independent scientific committee which advises the four nations, last considered the evidence for screening healthy men with no symptoms for prostate cancer in 2020 and concluded that screening was not recommended.  This is because of the poor ability of the prostate specific antigen (PSA) test to predict cancer, which could lead to unnecessary tests that carry risks of potentially life-changing harm. Additionally, some prostate cancers may not produce elevated PSA levels, leading to false-negative results that provide false reassurance. The UK NSC has commissioned a prostate cancer screening evidence review and cost effectiveness model, and it is expected to review this work during 2025. The UK NSC process will include a public consultation. The UK Health Departments, through the National Institute of Health and Care Research (NIHR), have invested in a significant programme of research to address the evidence gaps in this important area. A clinical trial (called the TRANSFORM trial), which will compare the most promising tests and provide evidence about the best way to screen for prostate cancer, is due to start in early 2025.My department will continue to be guided by the UK NSC’s recommendations on proposals for screening programmes. Legislation is not required to implement a new screening programme. Anyone with symptoms which they believe may be related to the prostate gland should contact their GP.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the steps his Department is taking to prepare for adverse winter weather conditions in the Derry City and Strabane District Council area.
answer_text: Prior to the start of each winter service season, my Department carries out a significant amount of pre-planning to ensure a state of readiness. Staffing arrangements have been finalised, pre-season checks on winter service equipment are complete and salt stocks have been replenished. My Department commits significant resources to its winter service programme and every night, from the end of October, up to 300 people will be on standby ready to salt main roads, helping drivers across the north to cope with the wintry conditions. Contracts are also in place to enable contractors and farmers to help to clear roads during periods of prolonged snow. While my Department targets the limited resources available for winter service on the busier main through routes, salt bins and grit piles are provided for use by the public, on a self-help basis, on other routes adopted or maintained by my department, which do not qualify for inclusion on the gritting schedule. Currently, contractors are in the process of refreshing grit piles throughout the Derry City and Strabane District Council area.In addition, my Department has an arrangement with Derry City and Strabane District Council to salt city and town centre footways during times of prolonged ice and snow, and the council will be contacted to ensure continuity of this service for the incoming winter season should conditions dictate.Our routine maintenance work to provide an annual clean to all DfI gullies in the Derry City and Strabane District Council area is also on-going alongside the routine cleaning of roadside channels and open drains and will continue throughout the winter season.In preparation for and during flood events local staff will utilise available resources to support community resilience groups in flood risk areas and respond, where possible, to requests for assistance from the public where property has suffered or is threatened by flooding. Departmental depots are fully stocked with sandbags for temporary flood protection which will be distributed directly when required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the funding support his Department currently provides for grassroots football clubs.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Football Fund is a flagship Executive commitment to invest in our local footballing infrastructure.  I have committed to moving this commitment forward quickly and so on 8 May 2024 I approved a policy framework to provide the basis to take the Programme forward. My officials are currently developing policy proposals for grassroots football clubs. Sport NI, an arm’s length body of my Department, has committed funding for grassroots clubs in 2023-24 and 2024-25.  As this is an operational matter, I sought details from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“In 2024/25 Sport NI have committed capital lottery funding to the following football clubs via the Renewable Energy Fund:Barn United Youth Football Club - £5,861Greenisland Football Club - £12,755Moyola Park Football Club - £18,627“These commitments were made on 1st November 2024, to date there has been no payments made.“In 2023/24 Sport NI committed £225,000 capital exchequer funding to Armagh City, Banbridge and Craigavon Borough Council under the Your School Your Club programme for developing a 3Gplaying pitch to the relevant National Governing Body (NGB) standards that is fully compliant with current legislation with a traditional build changing unit at Banbridge High School. To date £202,500 has been spent.“The Your School Your Club project at Banbridge High School will have a direct impact for grass roots football clubs upon completion of the facility.“Sport NI has launched Project Creating Opportunities. Details on this funding programme including eligibility criteria are available at https://www.sportni.net/funding/creating-opportunities/ Sport NI would welcome applications from grassroots football clubs and organisations.”

answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide an update on the benefits arising from local engagement at the International Investment Summit 2024.
answer_text: The International Investment Summit was an opportunity for us to make clear that our economy is open for investment.  It allowed us to be visible to a wide range of global investment stakeholders, and to engage directly with companies who might consider investing here, as well as those who already have a presence.  We were able to hear the latest developments from the Government on their plans to support investment and remove barriers.						      It also gave us the opportunity to reinforce the message of why this place is a great place to invest.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline whether her Department will consider introducing legislation to increase penalties for death by dangerous driving.
answer_text: I plan to bring forward a Sentencing Bill during this Assembly mandate which will include increasing the maximum penalties for those convicted of offences causing death or serious injury by dangerous driving or careless driving while under the influence of drink or drugs or careless driving and refusing to provide a specimen. The proposed maximum penalty for these offences will increase from 14 years to 20 years but where an offender has a previous conviction for these offences it is proposed the maximum sentence available will be a discretionary life sentence. The minimum period of mandatory disqualification from driving for these offences will increase from 2 to 4 years, and for a repeat offender, from 3 to 6 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the Western Health and Social Care Trust have sought any additional resources for respite provisions for children, young people and adults with severe disabilities.
answer_text: The Department of Health (DoH) fully recognise the need to support carers in their caring role.  Support can take the form of short breaks/respite support and a Carers Assessment.  All requests for short breaks are assessed and managed by HSC Trusts based on individual need, with statutory and independent sector care homes facilitating both planned and emergency short breaks.  Short breaks within adult services are monitored via quarterly data returns to the DoH SPPG Information Department.  In addition, Carers Assessments are monitored via the Directed Statutory Function (DSF) process.  The DSF process provides annual formal reporting from HSC Trusts to the DoH across programme of care.   Short break activity in Children’s services is monitored by the DSF process. Regionally short break availability for young people with disability is a recognised pressure across the HSC Trusts.  There has been an increase in children & young people reported to have disability and there has been an increasing demand for access to short breaks & respite services.  A Children with Disability Urgent Summit was convened on 26.09.2024 to consider potential options in respect of improving the regional access to Short Break provision.  It was agreed that each HSC Trust would work to develop Short Term Actions for DoH consideration.   The Western Trust have submitted a range of proposals covering both short term and medium-term options for bed based short breaks provision.  The DoH are currently reviewing the options provided.In adult services there is reduced capacity within the care home sector to support short breaks provision due to the pressures to facilitate hospital discharges, with some Trusts reporting limited bed availability generally.  The Western Trust are challenged in terms of care home availability, particularly in the southern sector of the Trust.  Health and Social Care Trusts can offer alternatives to bed based care such as day care, a sitting service in the person’s own home or a Direct Payment.  In addition, within Older People’s and Learning Disability services there is the availability of Shared Lives which provides alternative forms of support to the cared for person and ultimately their carer.  The service is well established in the learning disability programme and recently launched by the Department of Health in older people’s services.The Adult Physical Disability service in the Western Trust have raised the issue of cost pressures for caring for individuals with complex needs in community settings including in care homes but have not sought any additional funding specifically for short breaks.  The Trust has provided reassurance that they support individuals with complex disabilities primarily using one off and recurring Direct Payments, and on occasion, residential/nursing placements if they are available in the locality.  They advise that they do not have unmet need in this area.With regards to the Learning Disability service in th

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether there are any plans to upgrade Londonderry Courthouse.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service (NICTS) is currently progressing a major capital project to modernise Bishop Street Courthouse in Londonderry, subject to business case approval and funding availability. This project will explore a range of options to improve User Experience, Resilience, Value for Money and Sustainability, consistent with the Aims of the Agency’s Estate Strategy. The project is at an early stage, however NICTS intends to commence procurement of an Integrated Consultancy design team before the end of 2024 to develop the project brief and initial design solutions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps he is taking to re-establish a paediatric pathology service.
answer_text: It is important for any parent going through the terrible experience of losing a child to have the option of a post-mortem examination to help provide answers about their loss, and that they are fully supported at all stages of this process if they decide to proceed.  The current service for hospital-consented post-mortems for Northern Ireland families is provided by Alder Hey Children’s Hospital in Liverpool, since 3 January 2019. Whilst my first preference would be the restoration of a dedicated paediatric pathology service for Northern Ireland, this remains incredibly challenging as the services are under pressure across the UK and globally due to a shortage of paediatric pathologists.I remain very keen to explore the feasibility of an all-island approach to these services in the longer term that might mitigate the need for infant remains to be transported overseas.  I had a number of conversations with Minister Donnelly about the possibility of future co-operation on this issue and I recently raised this with the new Irish Health Minister Jennifer Carroll MacNeill TD.  These conversations have been very positive. In the meantime, my priority is to ensure that bereaved parents continue to have access to a high-quality post-mortem service that provides the medical answers as well as the care and support that families need.  Access has therefore been secured by a further extension of these arrangements with Alder Hey.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans his Department has for further capital works at Altnagelvin Hospital, Londonderry.
answer_text: Capital works for Altnagelvin Phase 5.1, which includes the construction of a new paediatric wing, are continuing.The Western Health and Social Care Trust has a submitted proposal for Phase 5.2, including the redevelopment of the Emergency Department, the redevelopment of critical and unscheduled care and the expansion of theatre accommodation, which are included in the future capital plan.The Trust will be invited to submit a business case for this proposal once budget affordability is confirmed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of social houses that are planned to be built in Foyle in the next five years.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) is currently formulated on a 3-year basis. The information below is therefore provided on this basis. In the Foyle Parliamentary Constituency:There have been 31 social housing completions to-date in 2024/25. There are currently 1,097 social housing units under construction of which 165 will complete in the 2024/24 programme year. There are 1,758 social housing units programmed to start as part of the Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) 2024/25 – 2027/28. 505 of which are due to start in 2024/25.Please note that the delivery of the SHDP is subject to Budget availability. Programmed schemes can be lost or slip to future programme years for a variety of reasons e.g. relating to delays in acquiring sites and/or failure to secure Planning Approval. Additional schemes can also be added to the SHDP ‘in-year’ through the Competitive Land, Design & Build procurement route and/or the purchase of Existing Satisfactory / Off-the-Shelf properties. The programme formulation process for the SHDP 2025/26 – 2027/28 is currently underway, and further schemes may be added in the Foyle Parliamentary Constituency in due course, subject to Departmental/Ministerial Approval”.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will consider making improvements to the existing Newbuildings to Strabane section of the A5, in light of the recent Planning Appeals Commission inquiry report.
answer_text: My officials continually monitor safety across the road network, including the existing A5, to ensure the current measures are appropriate. This includes regular review of PSNI collision statistics and their causation factors along this route.At particular locations where there may be common causation factors, officials will investigate options to introduce targeted road safety interventions ensuring that these measures will deliver most road safety benefits.Last year my department invested over £500,000 in safety related projects on the A5.In 2024, we are also spending a further £1m renewing the white lines, cats eyes, signs and drainage between Omagh and Derry City.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the School Enhancement Programme works at Lisneal College, Londonderry.
answer_text: Lisneal College, Londonderry has not been announced under my Department’s School Enhancement Programme.  However, the Education Authority is currently progressing a minor capital works scheme to deliver an extension to the school to provide three general classrooms and a drama/dance/lecture theatre.  Works in respect of this scheme commenced on site on 30 September and are anticipated to complete in April 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department's plans to roll-out dementia safeguarding scheme wristbands in the Foyle constituency.
answer_text: My Department recognises that people living with Dementia, and those who care for them, across Northern Ireland want to be assured that they are safe and that they can be appropriately assisted, if needed, when in public spaces.   My Department welcomes any new and innovative ideas that improve the lives of individuals living with Dementia in Northern Ireland. My officials have contacted both the Western Health and Social Care Trust and the Community Planning Department within Derry City and Strabane District Council.Derry City and Strabane District Council have commenced early discussions with my officials to explore the roll out of the Dementia safeguarding scheme wristbands within the Foyle Area.  The Western Health and Social Care Trust and Age Concern have agreed to work with my officials and with all councils and/or other interested parties about potential roll outs of the scheme across Northern Ireland.  Awareness of this initiative will also be promoted by the Health and Social Care Trusts through the Dementia Service Improvement Leads, the Dementia Navigator service, and other Dementia projects across the area.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice how her Department supports Victims Support NI (i) financially; and (ii) through accountability.
answer_text: Funding of £2,260,526 will be provided to Victim Support NI in this financial year to contribute to organisational costs for the delivery of support to victims and witnesses of crime through their Community Service, Criminal Injuries Compensation Service, Witness Service, Children and Young Persons Independent Sexual Violence Advocate Service, Sexual Offences Legal Adviser Service pilot and Hate Crime Advocacy Service.Grant agreements and a contract (Hate Crime Advocacy Service) are in place which outline the accountability requirements of the funding.  These include the requirement for Victim Support NI to provide my Department and/or the contract lead with regular financial, performance and corporate information.  Bilateral accountability meetings are held with Victim Support NI throughout the financial year, as well as any ad hoc meetings as required.  My Department also attends Victim Support NI’s Finance and Remuneration Committee meetings on an observer basis and is copied into the papers agreed at Victim Support NI’s Board meetings.

answer_text: The sale process for former Faughan Valley High School site closed on 7 June 2024.  Work on the legal agreements connected with the sale is ongoing with the successful bidder and we expect to complete the sale in the 2024/25 Financial Year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for his Department's assessment of traffic congestion on Limavady Road, Londonderry; and (ii) whether his Department will consider developing a traffic management plan to address residents' concerns.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of funding provided by his Department to the City of Derry Airport in each of the last five financial years.
answer_text: My Department has provided no funding directly to the City of Derry Airport in each of the last five financial years.In my statement to the Assembly on 1 October, launching the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, I confirmed that my Department is in the process of taking responsibility for the funding provided by Derry City and Strabane District Council to the City of Derry Airport.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he plans to bring forward a dedicated strategy and action plan to support the hospitality sector.
answer_text: I recognise the contribution of the hospitality sector to both our tourism offer and the wider economy.I have established a Tourism Partnership Board to support my Department in the co-design and development of a Tourism Action Plan to lay out a vision for the sustainable and inclusive growth of our valuable tourism sector. The first meeting of the Board took place last month with further meetings planned over the coming months.  To ensure a collaborative co-design approach, membership of the Partnership Board consists of representatives from both the tourism industry and the hospitality sector, alongside Executive Departments and local government. This will ensure that issues important to the hospitality sector will be considered, such as the need for a bespoke Skills Action Plan to address skills gaps, rewarding careers including competitive salaries, career pathways and equitable distribution of opportunities throughout the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the steps his Department is taking to ensure that the Local Economic Partnerships will be inclusive and representative of each area.
answer_text: Membership guidance will be provided, and parameters will be set to ensure that the Local Economic Partnerships reflect key stakeholders in the locality.  Councils will be required to submit to the Department for approval, their proposals for the structure and composition for partnerships in their area.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether her Department will conduct a review of the Small Business Rates Relief scheme with a view to providing further targeted support for businesses.
answer_text: Currently £250 million is paid in rates support to the non-domestic rating sector. This includes the Small Business Rate Relief Scheme which provides assistance to almost 30,000 small businesses who benefit from a reduction of between 20% to 50% on their rates liability.The rating system is the Executive’s only substantive revenue raising lever for public spending and the fiscal situation is well documented. Every pound provided in new rate relief has to be paid for either by other ratepayers in the system paying more, or by reduction in public spending. In terms of review work, I will be bringing forward short, medium and long-term rating policy proposals, including a more strategic longer-term policy approach, to the Executive for consideration later this month.It is my intention to make a statement to the Assembly on any changes to the rating system once the Executive has agreed the way forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the support his Department is providing to Translink to help support the night-time economy across the Derry City and Strabane District Council area.
answer_text: The current Departmental budget does not allow for any financial support for additional services. Any additional services would be an operational matter for Translink to provide within their current allocation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when his Department will introduce a scheme to address ongoing traffic issues at Caw roundabout, Londonderry.
answer_text: My Department has completed a preliminary design for the signalisation and upgrade of Caw Roundabout.  Development of a Department led scheme at this location will be subject to the availability of funding and priorities set by the Departments emerging Transport Plans. However, I can confirm that the upgrade and signalisation of Caw Roundabout is included in the planning conditions for the adjacent H30 development. The construction of the upgrade at Caw is currently conditioned on the occupation of the 301st dwelling.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps he is taking to bring further employment opportunities to the Foyle Constituency within the remit of his Department.
answer_text: I am committed to greater regional balance. I announced, on 08 October 2024, that my department has secured 1,000 new jobs as part of an expansion of services my Department delivers on behalf of the Department for Work and Pensions. Over 40% of these new jobs will be outside Belfast, with 270 posts based within the Foyle Constituency.I also recognise the significance of how supporting people into employment is a fundamental principle in tackling poverty.  My staff in the Jobs & Benefits offices in the North-West play a key role in supporting people in the Foyle Constituency to move towards and into work.  They work directly with employers in the Foyle area, from across all sectors of the economy, to help match employers and jobseekers through job fairs and bespoke employer recruitment events.  My Department also provides funding for the Derry and Strabane Labour Market Partnership, which has been operational since 2021/22. This Partnership works collaboratively to improve labour market conditions within the Foyle constituency and to connect employers and employees. In addition, my Department has a lead role in the Cross Border Partnership for Employment Services working in partnership with the Department of Social Protection to share and promote employment opportunities on both sides of the border, and offer support, advice and guidance to employers, jobseekers and cross border workers to help improve employment opportunities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the success of the Resilience Education Assisting Change to Happen programme in schools.
answer_text: The Resilience in Education Assisting Change to Happen (REACH) programme, led by the Education Authority Youth Service, supports schools to improve emotional health and wellbeing outcomes for pupils (6-19 year olds), whilst also providing support for teachers and parents.		Almost 8,000 young people engaged with or were supported by the programme in the 2023/24 academic year.  The programme is meeting its objectives, with a core focus on promoting good mental health amongst children and young people, reducing emotional difficulties or behavioural challenges and building capacity amongst education staff. Recent case studies demonstrate positive feedback from both pupils and teachers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in order to identify liver disease cases more effectively, to detail the steps his Department is taking to increase the usage of existing FibroScan machines in each Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The current pathway for people identified in primary care with suspected liver disease is referral to secondary care services in their local Trust. A pilot initiative aimed at refining the local pathway for services provided in Belfast Trust, specifically around non-alcohol fatty liver disease (NAFLD) has been trialled. This involved:A GP identifying a patient with NAFLD and undertaking initial screening and then sending a referral to the Hepatology ServiceThe referral is triaged by a consultant and is either forwarded for a consultant clinical appointment or if appropriate to a nurse led clinicIf the appointment is triaged to a nurse led clinic a fibroscan is undertaken and the nurse can provide lifestyle advice regarding NAFLDIf there are any abnormal findings on the scan then the patient is referred on to a consultant clinic, if not then they are discharged back to their GP with appropriate advice.Due to the success of this pilot the Regional Liver Unit is now applying the criteria to NAFLD referrals from all Belfast Trust practices and is also exploring the implications for similar pathway arrangements in other Trust areas.In extending the pathway arrangements, consideration will need to be given to fibroscan capacity required in other Trusts. There is a monthly meeting of the NI Hepatology Forum which includes representation from each Trust and the discussions on pathway refinement and fibroscan capacity can take place at this forum.However, any decision to roll out the pathway and increase the use of the fibroscan machines in other Trust areas would need to take account of any resource implications if this cannot be accommodated through service reconfiguration.  Additional resources, if required, would be subject to identification of funding which has not, to date, been factored in to financial projections in the already constrained health budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how the Road Safety Strategy for Northern Ireland to 2030 will support families bereaved through road traffic collisions.
answer_text: The devastation each and every road death leaves in its wake is immense and as road users we must all do everything we can – both individually and collectively – to reduce road deaths.  I take road safety issues seriously and my focus is to reduce the number of people killed and seriously injured on our roads. Officials from my Department have provided road safety support and resources to organisations such as the charities Life After and Road Safe NI.  Life After is a charity based in the North of Ireland, delivering support services to family and friends of those who have lost their lives on our roads. Road Safe NI are a charity promoting road safety reaching out to local communities and vulnerable road users through a network of local committees. In conjunction with The Road Ahead Support Group, Road Safe NI provides support for those affected by road traffic collisions. My Department has, for several years, sponsored the Lifetime Achievement Award at the Northern Ireland Annual Road Safety Awards organised by Road Safe NI.  I have recently met with representatives from both groups, and we agreed that reducing the death toll on our roads was a multi-departmental responsibility, along with external bodies such as Road Safe NI and Life After, and that collaboration was key to achieving the goal of a 50% reduction in road deaths by 2030.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department is considering introducing legislation to address dangerous driving behaviours.
answer_text: The Road Traffic (NI) Order 1995 provides for the offence of dangerous driving.  I have no plans to legislate further in respect of that offence however, the Road Safety Strategic Action Plan 2024/25 includes a number of strategic interventions with proposals to further address driving behaviours including in the areas of drink and drug driving and use of mobile phones.
